Strategic Engagement Models

“I do not trade hours for dollars. I trade conviction for outcomes.”

01. The Roadmap (Strategic Audit)

Focus: 0-to-1 Thesis & Market Positioning Designed for early-stage founders or funds needing a second opinion on "impossible" technical bets. A high-intensity, 4-week deep dive into your technical stack and market inevitability.

Scope: Infrastructure audit, non-consensus thesis validation, and competitive moat analysis.
Outcome: A 12-month Execution Blueprint identifying the "bottleneck primitives" your team must solve to win.
Best for: Seed-stage teams preparing for a Series A or "Hard Tech" pivots.
02. The Architecture (Operational Sprint)

Focus: Designing the Digital Backbone A quarterly engagement where I act as a Strategic Architect. I don’t just give advice; I help design the protocols, security frameworks, and AI integration layers that form your company’s "Sovereign Backbone."

Scope: Protocol design (Blockchain/Fintech), AI implementation strategy, and Cybersecurity hardening.
Outcome: Functional Technical Specs and the implementation of core infrastructure rails.
Best for: Teams building complex systems in Cyber, Infra, or Generative Content.
03. The Partner (Fractional Venture Partner)

Focus: Long-term Stewardship & Scaling A high-conviction, equity-heavy partnership. I join your "Inner Circle" as a Fractional Partner or Board Advisor, bridging the gap between technical engineering and venture-scale growth.

Scope: Senior technical hiring (First 5 engineers/designers), fundraising narrative development, and board-level strategy.
Outcome: Sustained Technical & Cultural Scaling through the growth phase.
Best for: High-growth startups needing an experienced "Operating Partner" without the full-time C-Suite overhead.
How to implement this in Replit (The Prompt)

When you send this to the Replit Agent, use this prompt to ensure the styling matches your minimalist "Terminal" vibe:

"I want to add the 3-tier 'Engagement Models' to my advisory.html page.

Layout Instructions:

Display the three tiers (The Roadmap, The Architecture, The Partner) in a vertical stack with plenty of whitespace between them.
Numbering: Use large, low-opacity numbers (01, 02, 03) as background elements behind each title.
Typography: Keep the titles in a bold, white Sans-Serif (Geist/Inter) and the body text in a readable, muted-gray Serif (Charter/Newsreader).
Interaction: On hover, the entire section for a specific tier should have a very subtle 'glow' or a change in the border-left color (use the accent color from the Home page).
The 'CTA' Button: At the bottom of each tier, add a minimalist link that says: '[ Initiate Engagement ]' which scrolls down to the inquiry form."
A "G Lauzier" Touch:

Since you are covering Digital Art and Entertainment, you might want to call the "Architecture" tier "The Creative Engine" for clients in those specific sectors.
